channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Putin lands in China for meeting with 'dear friend' Xi
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin smiled as he greeted Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi at the airport in Beijing.

media:group.media:description: Russian President Vladimir Putin greets Chinese officials, including Foreign Minister Wang Yi after he landed in Beijing.

item.title: Far-right Israeli minister accuses ICC of making 'secret' request to arrest him
item.description: Reports have been circulating that the ICC is preparing to issue warrants against Israel's far-right finance minister, Bezalel Smotrich.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Doran

media:group.media:description: Israeli finance minister Bezalel Smotrich says ICC prosecutors are requesting to issue an arrest warrant against him.

item.title: Son arrested in Spain over billionaire fashion tycoon father's deadly 100m cliff fall
item.description: A family spokesperson confirmed Jonathan Andic was being questioned by investigators. His father, Isak Andic, died in December 2024.

media:group.media:description: Jonathan Andic arrives at court escorted by police, where he is expected to be questioned.

item.title: Australia is wasting $1 billion on car parks nobody uses, report finds
item.description: Australia is wasting more than $1 billion each year building off-street car parks that end up sitting unused and unwanted, a Grattan report has found.
item.dc:creator: Kenji Sato

media:group.media:description: The report found the number of car parks far exceeded the number of cars in Australia's capital cities.

item.title: Two Italian divers' bodies recovered from underwater cave
item.description: Finnish divers recovered the bodies after they were located deep inside an underwater cave in the Maldives.

media:group.media:description: The plan is to recover the remaining two bodies on Wednesday.

item.title: Inquest hears elderly man who died was given another person's medication
item.description: An 82-year-old South Australian man died after he ingested prescription medication that was meant for another person and had been incorrectly labelled, a coronial inquest is told.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Carbone

media:group.media:description: The incorrectly labelled medication was collected from Chemist Warehouse pharmacy at the Parabanks Shopping Centre.

item.title: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth dies aged 91
item.description: Peter Hollingworth was the first and only cleric to serve as governor-general, before his resignation in 2003 amid his handling of historical church abuse scandals.
item.dc:creator: Ciara Jones

media:group.media:description: Former governor-general Peter Hollingworth has died.

item.title: Father in critical condition as police probe whether altercation with hoons led to assault
item.description: Geraldton police investigate whether complaints about hooning were behind an alleged assault that left a father-of-three in a critical condition.
item.dc:creator: Piper Duffy

media:group.media:description: Father-of-three Brodie Stokes (right) is in a critical condition after an assault in a Geraldton suburb.

item.title: Undersea volcanic eruption visible from space
item.description: The Titan Ridge Volcano is erupting in the Bismarck Sea, sending ash and smoke into the sky and prompting authorities to warn of possible tsunamis in the area.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Martin and Dana Kirimof

media:group.media:description: Experts were watching the eruption closely despite its remote location.

item.title: Aussie great-grandmother to compete at Comm Games
item.description: At 75 years old, Jacky Hudson will tread an unlikely path for a great-grandmother after earning selection in Australia's Commonwealth Games lawn bowls squad.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Stewart

media:group.media:description: Jacky Hudson, 75, is headed to Glasgow with the Jackaroos.

item.title: Man charged after alleged shotgun incident in Hobart suburbs
item.description: A 27-year-old man has been charged with a raft of offences following an alleged shooting incident in Hobart's southern suburbs.

media:group.media:description: The Westpac rescue helicopter was used in the search for the man.

item.title: Planned upgrades to ageing submarine fleet scaled back
item.description: A massive program of upgrades to Australia's aging fleet of Collins Class submarines, aimed at keeping them in the water a decade longer, is being scaled back.
item.dc:creator: Tom Lowrey

media:group.media:description: The Collins Class submarine fleet will need to surface more frequently than nuclear-powered boats.

item.title: 'Silence is not consent': Mother raped by massage therapist
item.description: A Queensland massage therapist has been imprisoned for raping a client who says "silence is not consent" and that the "degrading" experience devastated her emotionally, physically and financially.
item.dc:creator: Madeline Grace

media:group.media:description: Caroline Clarke was raped by a massage therapist in December 2024.

item.title: Heat back CA plan to open BBL season in India
item.description: Cricket Australia has spoken with BBL franchises about a plan to open this season's BBL with a showdown in India.

media:group.media:description: The Brisbane Heat are interested in taking a BBL game to India.

item.title: Snowtown killer's suppression order lifts after 25 years
item.description: Snowtown killer James Vlassakis's image can be revealed after South Australia's highest court lifted a suppression order made 25 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Eva Blandis

media:group.media:description: A court sketch of Vlassakis from 2026.

item.title: Top BOM exec who led bungled $96M website revamp departs role
item.description: Peter Stone was acting CEO when the BOM launched its new website, which was widely criticised for its costs and poor design.
item.dc:creator: Tessa Flemming

media:group.media:description: Peter Stone was the Bureau of Meteorology's acting CEO during the website rollout.

item.title: Rain 'more than double' forecast drenches Gold Coast amid BOM accuracy concerns
item.description: As flash flooding from record-setting rainfall recedes, some south-east Queensland residents feel that Bureau of Meteorology forecasts fell short of what was experienced.

media:group.media:description: Rain has caused roads to be flooded, including Tarata Road at Guanaba.

item.title: Tribunal rules in favour of landlord in first test of new pet rental rules
item.description: A landlord's decision to deny a tenant's request to keep a 6-month-old rescue kitten in a unit has been upheld, despite new Tasmanian laws intended to make it easier to keep pets in rentals.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie Heard

media:group.media:description: British Shorthair kitten generic.

item.title: Refunds but no price caps for ripped-off older Australians
item.description: Support at Home program price caps, due to start in July, have been deferred indefinitely. Hundreds of thousands of older people rely on the federal government program, which provides help with cleaning, showering and clinical supports like nursing.

media:group.media:description: Home care price caps to stop older Australians being overcharged have been deferred indefinitely.

item.title: RBA warns Australia risks slipping into a '1990s recession' as oil shock mounts
item.description: The Reserve Bank is growing increasingly anxious about price pressures building in the economy and its chief economist has raised the risk of a recession in post-budget remarks.
item.dc:creator: David Taylor

media:group.media:description: Sarah Hunter has spoken at an investment gathering in Sydney.

item.title: Economist blames ACT's fiscal deterioration entirely on 'policy decisions'
item.description: An independent economist warns Canberrans will need to pay higher taxes and charges if the ACT government continues the same amount of spending on infrastructure and services.
item.dc:creator: Monte Bovill

media:group.media:description: Economist Saul Eslake says the ACT government spent more on delivering services and providing new infrastructure without raising sufficient additional revenue.

item.title: Church pulls out all the stops for its pipe organ restoration
item.description: A restoration project which saw a church organ deconstructed pipe-by-pipe is now ready to play again.
item.dc:creator: Kemii Maguire

media:group.media:description: John Standley plays an organ pipe, removed from its resting place in the church.

item.title: Ex-GP charged with 148 counts of rape and sexual assault
item.description: A former Brisbane doctor has been charged with 148 counts of rape and sexual assault following an extensive police investigation.
item.dc:creator: Eden Gillespie

media:group.media:description: Stan Theodoros is facing charges of rape and sexual assault.

item.title: Death penalty rates at 40-year high globally, Amnesty International says
item.description: A crackdown in Iran drove a surge in the number of people executed worldwide in 2025, but China remains the "leading executioner," human rights group Amnesty International says.
item.dc:creator: Doug Dingwall

media:group.media:description: Activists attend a candlelight vigil in February 2025 against the execution of a man in Singapore for drug trafficking.

item.title: How planes shot down decades ago could be central to Trump's plans in Cuba
item.description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel Bermúdez says Cuba's right to defend itself should not be used by the US as justification for a potential war.
item.dc:creator: Luke Cooper

media:group.media:description: Brothers to the Rescue planes flew over the Florida Straits in the 1990s to drop supplies to Cubans fleeing their country.
